Battery SipJuice v0.5.0 brings richer long-term battery insights, multi-battery support, smarter alerts, and refreshed Linux desktop integration.

## Highlights

- Added multi-battery support with independent history and health data for every installed battery.
- Added charging-session history, including charge range, duration, estimated energy, power, temperature, and input-source information.
- Added long-term battery-health trends with daily and post-charge snapshots, a smoothed capacity-health curve, and wear-per-cycle estimates.

## Monitoring and reminders

- Added configurable high- and low-temperature notifications with hysteresis to prevent repeated alerts near a threshold.
- Added sustained abnormal-drain notifications with an adjustable power threshold and sliding-window detection to ignore brief spikes.
- Migrated existing single-battery history automatically to the new per-battery storage format.

## Interface and desktop integration

- Improved system accent-color matching by reading the active GTK theme directly, with `gsettings` retained as a fallback.
- Replaced native battery and input-source selectors with accessible custom controls that support keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes.
- Made Follow System the default accent-color option and refined the built-in Lava Orange and Vivid Blue themes.
- Redesigned the application and tray icons with cleaner geometry, gradients, subtle shadows, and sharper Linux rendering.

## Packaging and project updates

- Added automated x64 and ARM64 releases containing AppImage, Debian, and RPM packages.
- Added bilingual English and Chinese documentation with refreshed screenshots and build instructions.
- Changed the project license from MIT to Apache License 2.0 and added the corresponding NOTICE file.

Thank you for using Battery SipJuice.
